7.6       The court officer must inform—


(a)        the applicant under paragraph 7.4;


(b)        the person to be protected and P (if those persons are not the applicant);


(c)        the person who applied for the DAPO (if that person is not the applicant or the person to be protected); and


(d)        any other person directed by the court,


of the court’s action under paragraph 7.5.


7.7       The court may at any time direct that—


(a)        a person be joined as a party to the proceedings; or


(b)        that a party to the proceedings cease to be a party,


and such direction may be made by the court of its own initiative or on application under paragraph 7.4.


7.8       Where the court directs that a person be joined as a party or that a party cease to be a party under paragraph 7.7, it may give consequential directions as to—


(a)        service of the application for the DAPO and other relevant documents on the new party; and


(b)        the management of the proceedings.


7.9       This paragraph applies to proceedings for any variation or discharge of a DAPO as it applies to proceedings for a DAPO.


(For when a child should be joined as a party to proceedings generally see section 1 of Part 4 of Practice Direction 16A).


(Chapter 5 of Part 16 and Part 2 of Practice Direction 16A make general provision about the appointment of a litigation friend.)


## IV — SERVICE OF APPLICATIONS


## 8. Service of application for a DAPO on notice


8.1       Where a DAPO application is made on notice, subject to paragraph 8.2, the applicant must serve a copy of the DAPO application and supporting statement, together with notice of the hearing on—


(a)        P, personally;


(b)        the person to be protected (if that person is not the applicant); and


(c)        any other person directed by the court,


not less than 2 days before the date on which the application will be heard.


8.2       The court may direct that—


(a)        service is effected by an alternative method or at an alternative place; or


(b)        service be dispensed with.
